title: Code Review Fix-Up Plan v2 — Post-Fix Review of SNR8503M_gcc_full

fix_status.FIX2-001: FIXED — FLASH_CONFIG_VERSION bumped to 2; release note added inline in flash_config.h.
fix_status.FIX2-002: FIXED — s_last_error reset inside deadband branch alongside s_integral.
fix_status.FIX2-004: FIXED — rename macros now gated on SENSORLESS_ADAPTER_RENAME_TABLES, defined only in COMP_Sensoeless.c.
fix_status.FIX2-005: FIXED — motor_mode_detect early-exits once threshold reached on the valid path.
fix_status.FIX2-007: FIXED — CRC32 now uses 16-entry nibble lookup (~2x speedup, 64 B table).
fix_status.FIX2-008: FIXED — ARM_TOOLCHAIN env-var check also accepts .exe extension.
fix_status.FIX2-010: FIXED — comment in flash_config.h clarifies 0xB0/B1/B2 are NOT FlashContent fields.
fix_status.FIX2-011: FIXED — src/startup_canary.c with 4-word canary + startup_canary_check() called from main().
fix_status.FIX2-012: FIXED — u8FlagEnMotorRun checked first in both sensorless sub-state branches.
fix_status.FIX2-013: FIXED — timing budget documented in docs/protocol_codes.md.

fix_summary.build_status: All 7 profiles build clean. unified_full grew to 30,744 bytes (was 30,352 in v1 fixes; +392 B for v2 additions: canary array, diagnostic counters, features bitfield, nibble-lookup CRC table). Still 1.25 KB under 32 KB MDK-Lite cap.

scope: Re-review of all files modified in the iteration that applied fix-up-plan.json findings (FIX-001 through FIX-025). Looking for: (a) new issues introduced by the fixes, (b) issues missed in the first pass, (c) issues that became more visible/important after the fixes landed.
review_methodology: Self-review by Claude (no external agent this iteration). Lower yield expected — first pass already found 25 findings; remaining issues are subtler.

size_deltas_vs_pre_fix.hall_bipropellant_full: 28404 → 28536 (+132 B for CRC32 + improved Flash logic)
size_deltas_vs_pre_fix.unified_full: 30056 → 30352 (+296 B — CRC + BLDC_COMP_Input_Only + new flash logic)

post_fix_build_status.verdict: All within budget. unified_full has 1.6 KB headroom under 32 KB MDK-Lite cap.

findings.title: Adding crc field is a breaking change — old Flash configs invalidated
findings.issue: FIX-013 added a `uint32_t crc` field at the end of FlashContent_t. The CRC validation in flash_config_load() will reject any previously-saved config (which didn't have a CRC). Devices upgraded from a pre-fix firmware will lose their flash-stored PID gains and other tunings on first boot after the upgrade. The behavior (fall back to defaults) is correct but the user-visible result is silent loss of tunings.
findings.fix: Add a release note. Optionally bump FLASH_CONFIG_VERSION from 1 → 2 to make the schema change explicit; the version mismatch on old configs already triggers default-restore.
findings.verification: Test: flash pre-fix firmware → write 0x81=99 → save → flash new firmware → read 0x81 should return 100 (default), not 99.

findings.title: Deadband resets s_integral but not s_last_error — derivative kick on re-engage
findings.issue: When error enters deadband (abs <= 2), s_integral=0 but s_last_error is not reset. On exit from deadband, the first tick computes `deriv = error - s_last_error` using a stale s_last_error from before the deadband. This produces a derivative kick proportional to the cumulative error change during the settled period.
findings.fix: Inside the deadband branch, also `s_last_error = error;` so derivative is zero on re-engage.
findings.verification: Bench: command position 100, motor settles to 100. Command 100 again (no error) — verify no transient. Then command 110 — verify smooth ramp, no spike at first tick.

findings.title: No compile-time verification that FlashContent_t is 4-byte aligned
findings.issue: FIX-005 added `__attribute__((aligned(4)))` to FlashContent_t. GCC honors this on most targets but the attribute can be silently ignored under unusual compiler flags. The Read_More_Flash call uses sizeof/4 — if alignment fails to take effect, last bytes are lost.
findings.fix: Add `_Static_assert((sizeof(FlashContent_t) & 3) == 0, "FlashContent_t must be 4-byte aligned for Read_More_Flash");` in flash_config.c just after the struct's first use.
findings.verification: Build clean. Intentionally break alignment (remove the attribute) — verify static assert fires.

findings.title: Motor_step_tab_* rename macros are visible to all consumers — footgun
findings.issue: The `#define Motor_step_tab_cw sns_Motor_step_tab_cw` macros are unconditional once sensorless_adapter.h is included AND MODULE_MOTOR_HALL=1. If any Hall-side code accidentally includes sensorless_adapter.h, its references to `Motor_step_tab_cw` are silently rewritten to the sensorless version. Currently safe because only sensorless code includes the header — but a future contributor adding `#include "sensorless_adapter.h"` to a Hall file would silently break Hall commutation.
findings.fix: Wrap the rename macros in `#ifdef SENSORLESS_ADAPTER_DEFINE_TABLES` and only define that token in src/COMP_Sensoeless.c just before the include. Or use prefixed names natively in COMP_Sensoeless.c at the cost of vendor-source modification.
findings.verification: Add `#include "sensorless_adapter.h"` to a Hall file (e.g., MC_Drive.c), build unified_full — Hall code should still use the [8] tables, not the [6] sensorless versions.

findings.title: motor_mode_detect samples for the full window even if early result is decisive
findings.issue: FIX-010 added majority voting — but the loop now runs the full sample_count iterations regardless of intermediate results. With a 500 ms detection window (max sensible) and a clearly-valid Hall signal in sample 1, we still wait 495 ms unnecessarily. Boot-time soft-start delay grows accordingly.
findings.fix: Optional optimisation: after a majority decision is mathematically certain (e.g., 7 valid samples out of first 7), exit early. Trivial: `if (valid_count >= threshold) break;` after the increment. Only do this on the 'valid' side; absence of valid samples must wait the full window.
findings.verification: Bench: time motor_mode_detect with valid Hall — should now complete in 35-40 ms (7 × 5 ms) instead of 50 ms.

findings.title: Invalid Hall_value silently disables CMP0 with no counter
findings.issue: Default case in the switch leaves CMP0 disconnected. If sensorless state machine ever calls with Hall_value outside 1-6 (bug or corruption), BEMF tracking silently fails. No diagnostic.
findings.fix: Add `static volatile uint32_t s_sensorless_bad_phase_count = 0;` and increment in default case. Expose via a new diagnostic code 0xB3 (paired with the future features-bitfield 0xB4 from FIX-022).
findings.verification: Inject bad value (force u8ControlStep=7) — counter increments. Normal operation — counter stays 0.

findings.title: Byte-by-byte CRC32 is slow on Cortex-M0
findings.issue: Reflected-polynomial CRC32 with 8 inner-loop iterations per byte = ~64 cycles/byte. For a ~120-byte FlashContent struct that's ~7,700 cycles ≈ 160 µs at 48 MHz. Called once at boot (load) and once per save. Acceptable but not great. A 256-entry table lookup would be ~10 cycles/byte (10x faster) at the cost of 1 KB of constant Flash. Or a 16-entry nibble lookup (~30 cycles/byte, 64 B table) is a good compromise.
findings.fix: Defer unless profiling shows it matters. Document in code comment as a known cost.
findings.verification: Profile with GPIO toggle wrapping fc_crc32 call.

findings.title: ARM_TOOLCHAIN env var check doesn't handle Windows .exe extension
findings.issue: `[ -x "${ARM_TOOLCHAIN}/arm-none-eabi-gcc" ]` succeeds on POSIX but fails on Windows MSYS if the binary is `arm-none-eabi-gcc.exe`. Workaround: PATH check via `command -v` does honor .exe, so the second fallback path catches this. Effective on Windows; ineffective for the explicit env-var path.
findings.fix: Add `.exe` suffix check: `if [ -x "${ARM_TOOLCHAIN}/arm-none-eabi-gcc" ] || [ -x "${ARM_TOOLCHAIN}/arm-none-eabi-gcc.exe" ]`. Or check via `"$ARM_TOOLCHAIN/arm-none-eabi-gcc" --version >/dev/null 2>&1`.
findings.verification: Set `ARM_TOOLCHAIN=/c/Users/Dell/.platformio/.../bin` on Windows — first path should now succeed instead of falling through to PATH.

findings.title: Save-failed counter exported but no host-visible code
findings.issue: FIX-012 adds `flash_config_save_failed_count()` accessor and increments on Flash program failure, but no bipropellant code exposes it. Host can't tell if a save silently failed.
findings.fix: Register a new param 0xB5 'flash_save_failed_count' (R) in protocol_params_flash.c. Read-only u8.
findings.verification: Simulate Flash failure (e.g., write to write-protected sector by misconfiguring linker temporarily). Verify 0xB5 reads > 0.

findings.title: Field comments still mention '0xB0 — gate flag' for HoverboardEnable but 0xB0 was reassigned to motor_mode_active
findings.issue: Comment on `HoverboardEnable` field says `/* 0xA0 — gate flag */` (correct). But anyone scanning comments quickly might confuse with motor_mode_active 0xB0 nearby. Documentation/code alignment.
findings.fix: Add a one-line note that codes 0xB0/B1/B2 are NOT FlashContent fields — they're motor_mode globals registered separately.

findings.title: No automated regression test for FIX-001 (startup .data copy)
findings.issue: FIX-001 was a silent corruption bug. The fix is now in place but if someone refactors startup.S in the future, the bug could regress invisibly. A simple canary in .data would catch it.
findings.fix: Add to a startup-validation .c file: `volatile uint32_t __data_canary[4] __attribute__((used)) = {0xCAFE0001, 0xCAFE0002, 0xCAFE0003, 0xCAFE0004};` Then at the top of main() (or in motor_mode_detect for visibility), check all 4 values and set a fault flag if mismatched.
findings.verification: Build with intentionally-broken startup — verify fault flag set. With correct startup, no fault.

findings.title: Sensorless branches in sub-state handlers don't gate on Motor.BLDC.u8FlagEnMotorRun
findings.issue: Hall sub-state handlers (M1M1_RunStartup, M1M1_RunSpin) check `if(Motor.BLDC.u8FlagEnMotorRun == 0) M1M1_SwitchRunStop();` at the top. Sensorless branches skip this check — they call the sensorless functions directly without verifying the run flag. If a host writes run_enable=0 while in MODE_SENSORLESS, the motor doesn't stop cleanly (the closed-lib functions might keep commutating until the next pass).
findings.fix: Add `if (Motor.BLDC.u8FlagEnMotorRun == 0) { M1M1_SwitchRunStop(); return; }` at the top of each sensorless branch.
findings.verification: Bench: enable motor in sensorless mode, send 0x09=0, verify motor stops within 1 ms.

findings.title: protocol_drain_rx in 10ms slot — TX response latency now up to 10 ms after RX
findings.issue: Moving protocol calls into the 10ms slot reduces CPU jitter (FIX-019 goal) but adds up to 10 ms latency to the response of a bipropellant command. Most bipropellant hosts are fine with this; some real-time control loops at ~50 Hz might notice.
findings.fix: Document the latency budget in docs/protocol_codes.md. If hosts need <10ms round-trip, move drain back to 1ms slot but cap the byte-count per call (e.g., max 8 bytes / 1ms call).
findings.verification: Profile: time from RX-IRQ to first TX byte. Should be < 11 ms (1 ms scheduler granularity + ≤ 10 ms slot wait + processing).

must_fix_before_release: FIX2-002 (deadband derivative kick — affects position-control accuracy)
FIX2-001 (document the CRC breaking change in release notes)

qualitative_assessment.code_quality_improvement: Significant. The HIGH-severity bugs from v1 (data corruption via startup, dropped param registrations, stub for critical function) are all fixed. Remaining issues are subtler — ergonomic concerns, edge cases, minor diagnostics gaps.
qualitative_assessment.no_HIGH_findings_in_v2: Encouraging — the v1 fixes did not introduce any new critical bugs. All v2 findings are MEDIUM or LOW.
qualitative_assessment.build_stability: All 7 profiles continue to build with no warnings or errors. Size growth is bounded and well-explained (CRC + Input_Only logic).
areas_still_weak: Sensorless verification — BLDC_COMP_Input_Only is now implemented but completely untested on hardware. The vendor port is mechanical; whether SYS_AFE_REG1 bit positions are correct for our specific board variant is unverified.
No host-side unit tests for the protocol layer — would catch parameter handler bugs without hardware.
Phase NN comments accumulated through 13 phases — readability noise.
Motor_step_tab rename trick (FIX2-004) is clever but fragile under future maintenance.
qualitative_assessment.ready_for_hardware_test: YES — after fixing FIX2-002 (derivative kick on deadband exit, 2 min fix). Other items are post-test polish.

notes: This v2 review found NO HIGH-severity issues, which is a strong sign that v1 fixes were applied carefully. The original 6 HIGH findings (startup ASM, pool overflow, ISR race, debounce, alignment, stub) are all genuinely resolved.

FIX-001 (startup .data copy) was the single most impactful fix — likely explains any past flaky behavior in GCC builds. Worth bench-testing once more with the corrected startup to confirm motor behavior matches the Keil/ARMCC build.
The new MEDIUM findings are mostly about robustness and design ergonomics rather than runtime correctness. They can be addressed at leisure.
If hardware testing reveals new issues (e.g., BLDC_COMP_Input_Only register bits wrong for SNR8503M-specific board variant), those would warrant a fix-up-plan-3.json.
